variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Global efforts slowly reduce malaria cases over many decades, but gains remain fragile and frequently reverse, leaving eradication a distant hope.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Global efforts gradually suppress malaria over several decades, achieving partial control that requires constant costly maintenance to prevent resurgence.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: Global efforts eradicate malaria after a prolonged multi-decade campaign, though residual risks of re-emergence demand ongoing vigilance and sustained investment.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: Global efforts eradicate malaria within a single decade, establishing durable protections that make resurgence highly unlikely.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Global efforts swiftly eradicate malaria within just a few years, with robust biological and institutional safeguards ensuring long-lasting elimination.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: Global efforts eradicate malaria in a rapid, decisive campaign that permanently and irreversibly eliminates the parasite worldwide, making any resurgence biologically impossible.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases in both speed (decades → years → rapid) and permanence (fragile/reversible → requires maintenance → durable → biologically irreversible). The core scenario—global efforts targeting malaria—is preserved throughout, with only the speed and durability of results varying.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because faster eradication means fewer people suffer and die from malaria during the campaign, and permanent irreversible elimination removes all future risk and ongoing costs, maximizing cumulative lives saved and resources freed.
